title: Fix the Simple Array Index Finder

statement.markdown: Implement a function called `findIndex` that takes an array of integers and a target integer as input and returns the index of the target integer in the array. If the target integer is not found, return -1. The function should use a simple and efficient approach.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be an array of integers and a target integer.
statement.outputFormat: Return the index of the target integer in the array, or -1 if not found.
statement.notes: The function should handle edge cases such as an empty array or a target integer that is not in the array.

examples.explanation: The target integer 3 is at index 2 in the array.
The target integer 6 is not in the array, so return -1.
An empty array does not contain any integers, so return -1.

hints.text: Use a simple loop to iterate over the array and find the target integer.

editorial.title: Editorial for Simple Array Index Finder
editorial.intuition: The function should use a simple and efficient approach to find the target integer in the array.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: using a simple loop to iterate over the array and find the target integer.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution: using a simple loop to iterate over the array and find the target integer.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function correctly returns the index of the target integer in the array, or -1 if not found.
